patterns.decisionQuestion: Which status lane or card needs an advance, rerun, assignment, diagnostic review, or detail follow-up?
patterns.emptyStateCopy: No items are in this lane. New work appears here after it is created or moved by the previous step.

typography: Board H1 uses text-2xl or smaller; card titles use text-sm/text-base, metadata uses text-xs/text-sm.
density: At a desktop review viewport, first viewport shows lane status strip, multiple lanes, and multiple actionable cards.
Cards are compact enough to show status, owner/schedule, progress, and action without becoming large blank panels.
spacing: Use gap-4/gap-5 between lanes and cards; avoid default gap-6/space-y-6 on board pages.
Lane height and card density should adapt to viewport and content; do not hard-code fixed pixel columns just to match one screenshot.
borders: Use TaskCard/SurfaceCard with subtle Forge outlines; heavy dashed borders are only for empty lane states.
profileRegion: Use native AppLayout topbar profile and no custom board topbar.
rightRail: If a board has a context rail, it must not push lanes below the fold.

forbiddenShortcuts: Do not make the kanban board read-only.
Do not omit lane empty states.
Do not use a generic custom card shell instead of Forge TaskCard or SurfaceCard.
Do not hide detail or diagnostic links from failed/blocked cards.
Do not count a supporting workflow route as promoted without IA, page-pattern, visual rule, and screenshot acceptance.
